text:
Once upon a time, in a quaint little town called Everwood, there lived a man named Harold Finch. Harold was known among his neighbors for his eccentricities—notably, his habit of wearing suspenders everywhere. His life took a peculiar turn when he started having vivid dreams. Every night, in these dreams, Harold visited a strange old antique shop.

On one dreamy night, Harold entered the shop. The owner, a retired curator named Professor Abernathy, greeted him warmly. The shop was a riot of oddities: clocks that sang, maps that told stories, feathers that twinkled like stars. Harold picked up a feather that shimmered faintly pink.

As Harold browsed, the shelves groaned under the weight of oddities. Finally, he stopped at a corner where dozens of books formed a calendar. Each book bore a peculiar illustration—fish with faces, frogs with guns, chickens wearing tiny crowns. Harold read the titles: “The Chicken Chancellors of Far
